title: Cross-Tenant Audit Log

story.i_want_to: query the audit log across all tenants from a single endpoint, filterable by tenant, entity type, action, and date range
story.so_that: I can investigate platform-wide incidents, trace cross-tenant issues, and review superadmin actions without switching between tenant contexts

rationale: The standard audit endpoint (US-3.1) is tenant-scoped — each tenant can only see their own audit entries. For platform operations (investigating abuse, auditing superadmin actions, debugging cross-tenant issues), the superadmin needs visibility across all tenants. This endpoint surfaces the same audit data but without tenant-scoping, with an additional tenant_id filter for narrowing results.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/admin/audit returns paginated audit log entries across ALL tenants, including entries with tenant_id=NULL (superadmin actions). Requires superadmin API key.
Supports filter by tenant_id to narrow results to a specific tenant's entries.
Supports all filters from the standard audit endpoint: entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_type, actor_id, date range (from/to as ISO8601).
Each audit entry in the response includes the tenant_id and tenant name/slug for identification.
Results are ordered by inserted_at descending (most recent first). Supports page and page_size parameters.
Entries with tenant_id=NULL (superadmin actions performed without impersonation) are included when no tenant_id filter is applied.
Can filter by actor_type='superadmin' to see all actions performed by superadmin keys (both direct and impersonated).

test_cases.name: cross-tenant audit returns entries from all tenants
preconditions: Audit entries exist for both tenants and for superadmin (tenant_id=NULL)
expected_results: Returns entries from Tenant A, Tenant B, and superadmin (NULL tenant)
Each entry includes tenant_id and tenant_name (or null for superadmin entries)

test_cases.name: filter by tenant_id
preconditions: Audit entries exist for both tenants
expected_results: Response includes only Tenant A's audit entries
No entries from Tenant B or superadmin (NULL) appear

test_cases.name: filter by actor_type=superadmin shows impersonated actions
preconditions: Audit entries: one from tenant's own user, one from superadmin impersonating this tenant
expected_results: Response includes only entries where actor_type='superadmin'
Includes the impersonated action (has tenant_id set but actor_type='superadmin')
Excludes the tenant's own user action

test_cases.name: combined filters work correctly
preconditions: Audit entries with various entity_types, actions, and dates
expected_results: Returns only entries matching ALL filters simultaneously
Entries are for the specified tenant, entity_type=story, action=status_changed, after Jan 1 2026

test_cases.name: pagination works on cross-tenant audit
preconditions: 30 audit log entries across multiple tenants
expected_results: Entries are globally ordered by inserted_at descending

test_cases.name: null tenant_id entries included in unfiltered results
preconditions: Audit entry with tenant_id=NULL (e.g., superadmin direct action)
steps: GET /api/v1/admin/audit with superadmin API key (no tenant_id filter)
expected_results: Response includes the entry with tenant_id=NULL
tenant_name is null for that entry

technical_notes: Create AdminAuditController with index action. Route: GET /api/v1/admin/audit under the /admin scope.

This endpoint queries audit_log without tenant_id scoping. The superadmin's NULL tenant context means RLS policies should allow full read access. If RLS blocks NULL tenant reads, add a policy: ALLOW SELECT WHEN current_setting('app.current_tenant_id') IS NULL.
Join audit_log with tenants on audit_log.tenant_id = tenants.id (LEFT JOIN to include NULL tenant entries). Select tenant.name and tenant.slug alongside the audit entry.
Reuse the filter parsing logic from AuditController (US-3.1) but add the tenant_id filter parameter.
Consider creating a shared Audit.list_entries_admin/1 function that skips tenant scoping, alongside the existing tenant-scoped Audit.list_entries/2.

Performance: ensure the (tenant_id, inserted_at) index on audit_log supports the combined tenant_id + date range queries efficiently. The cross-tenant unfiltered query relies on the inserted_at index alone.
Response includes tenant context: {tenant_id: uuid|null, tenant_name: string|null, tenant_slug: string|null} alongside the standard audit entry fields.
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
